title: 离网太阳能电池储能系统选型模型
background:
2021 HiMCM
Problem A: Storing the Sun

Your team is helping to plan the use of solar power to provide electricity to a 1600 square-foot
home being built in a remote area. You need to plan for enough energy to support the energy
requirements of the home at night and on a cloudy day. You have done some research and found
that you can either pull energy from the grid (i.e. a power company) when your solar panels
aren’t producing enough, or use an energy storage system. As the house is in a remote area, the
cost of connecting to the grid is very expensive, so you decide to go off-the-grid and invest in
energy storage.

Background. An energy storage system allows you to capture electricity, store it as another form
of energy (battery, thermal, mechanical) and then have it available to use when needed. The
purpose of these storage units is to store energy produced during sunny daylight hours for
use when the solar panels do not produce enough energy for the demand (night or cloud
covered), or for storage and transfer of excess energy. Of the solar-powered homes that use an
energy storage system, most use some sort of battery. Some homeowners have only one large
battery, while others may use a “bank of batteries” (two or more batteries connected). Energy
storage can be expensive and so homeowners should choose a system that is appropriate for their
situation. Figure 1 shows the general concept of off-the-grid energy.

Figure 1: Off-the-Grid Energy System

In choosing an energy storage system there are many criteria to consider. Here are a few of the
most common decision criteria, as well as which battery specifications matter most for each
criterion.

  To power more of your home at once, you will want a battery with a high continuous

power rating.

  To power a more energy-intensive appliance (requiring more power in short bursts), your

battery should have a high instantaneous power rating.

  To run your home for a longer amount of time, look for a battery with a higher usable



capacity.
If you want to get the most out of every kilowatt-hour of electricity you put into your
battery, look for a battery with a higher round-trip efficiency.

| ©2021 by COMAP, Inc. | www.comap.com | www.mathmodels.org | @comapmath | info@comap.com |

 Additional considerations apply for the type of battery.

  Lead-acid (flooded or sealed) and lithium-ion batteries are ideal for a full-time, off-grid

supply of different levels of use.

  Lead-acid batteries have been around for a long time and are known for their low prices

and reliability.

  Lithium-ion batteries are more expensive, but require no maintenance. Other options are

nickel cadmium and flow batteries.

  Look for lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries (a type of lithium-ion battery) to get the

longest lifetime that you can cycle the most times.

  For the absolute highest safety rating possible (although they are all safe), look to LFP

batteries.

Requirements

1. Consider the 1600 square-foot off-the-grid home you are planning.

a. Analyze your solar-power storage requirements by first making a list of questions to
determine your energy needs. Discuss the range of possible answers to your questions. To get
you started, here are a few questions:

  How many people will be using energy in this home?
  What items in the home will need energy and how much energy will they need?
  When will people in the home use energy?

b. Use your analysis from part 1.a., along with the criteria and considerations from the
problem background and any other factors you consider important, to develop a
mathematical model or algorithm for choosing the “best” battery storage system for your off-
the-grid home.

c. Consider available battery storage options and use your model to choose the best option for
your off-the-grid home. The chart at the end of the problem statement provides several, but
not all, battery options. Discuss your choice.

2. Adjust and generalize your model from Requirement 1 so that it is adaptable to individual
needs and preferences to choose the best battery storage for any home. Discuss the changes you
make to your model. Evaluate your model.

3. Recently, researchers in Sweden discovered that cement could be used to store energy[1].
Concrete, which is made with cement, is used to build buildings, sidewalks, bridges, and
countless other structures.

a. Identify some of the advantages and disadvantages of using cement batteries to store solar
power. Describe how might you incorporate cement as a battery for your off-the-grid home
or for any home?

b. Determine and discuss the additional information you would need in order for you to
model and compare the use of cement batteries to currently available batteries for solar-
power storage. Note: You are NOT required to create the model.

| ©2021 by COMAP, Inc. | www.comap.com | www.mathmodels.org | @comapmath | info@comap.com |

 4. Write a one-page non-technical news article describing your solar power battery storage
decision model. Include in your article any recommendations for the future possibilities of a
cement battery.

Your PDF solution of no more than 25 total pages should include:

 One-page Summary Sheet.
 Table of Contents.
 Your complete solution.
 One-page Article.
 References list.

Note: The HiMCM Contest now has a 25-page limit. All aspects of your submission count
toward the 25-page limit (Summary Sheet, Table of Contents, Reference List and any
Appendices).

Glossary

Continuous Power Rating: the kilowatts (kW) or Amps (A) of power that the battery can
provide continuously (until the battery runs out of power).

Energy Storage System: a system that captures electricity, stores it in another form, and
then retrieves it for use at a later time.

Instantaneous Power Rating: the kilowatts (kW) or Amps (A) of power that the battery can
provide in short bursts (but not sustain over a long period).

Lifetime (of battery): the length of time your battery will continue working which is
measured by expected years of operation, expected throughput (kWh of use), and expected
number of cycles (charging and discharging).

Off-The-Grid: not using or depending upon public supply of electricity (or other utilities).

Round-Trip Efficiency: the number of units of electricity you’ll get out of a battery for
every unit you put into it measured in a percentage.

Usable Capacity: the amount of electricity, in kilowatt-hours (kWh) or Amp-hours (Ah) that
a battery is able to store and supply to your home.

Reference

[1] Zhang, Emma Q., and Luping Tang. 2021. "Rechargeable Concrete
Battery" Buildings 11, no. 3: 103. https://doi.org/10.3390/buildings11030103

steps.guide_style: 苏格拉底式追问，引导分点列举，不直接给答案
steps.deliverable: 子问题清单 + 关键约束
steps.title: 问题分析与重述
steps.prompt: 请阅读题目背景，用自己的话重述问题：你需要为一个1600平方英尺的离网住宅选择太阳能储能电池。列出至少3个关键决策因素（如成本、容量、效率等），并说明每个因素的重要性。

criteria.dim: 准确重述问题，明确离网住宅和储能需求
列出至少3个关键决策因素，并解释其重要性
逻辑清晰，表述完整

steps.reference_outline: 问题要求为1600平方英尺离网住宅选择最佳电池储能系统，需满足夜间和阴天用电需求。关键因素包括：成本（直接影响预算）、可用容量（决定能存储多少电）、连续功率（决定能同时驱动多少电器）、效率（影响实际可用电量）。这些因素共同决定电池是否满足家庭需求且经济可行。
steps.hint: 考虑家庭用电设备（如冰箱、空调、照明）的功率和用电时间，以及电池的规格参数。

steps.guide_style: 质疑每条假设的必要性与合理性
steps.title: 模型假设
steps.prompt: 列出至少5个合理的模型假设，并简要说明每个假设的理由。例如：假设家庭用电模式稳定、电池性能不随温度变化等。

criteria.dim: 列出至少5个假设
每个假设有合理的理由
假设不违背问题背景，且有助于简化模型

steps.reference_outline: 1. 家庭用电设备功率和用电时间固定，不考虑季节性变化。2. 电池充放电效率恒定，不考虑老化影响。3. 太阳能板发电量充足，仅需考虑储能。4. 电池成本仅考虑购买成本，不考虑安装和维护。5. 所有电池均能安全运行，不考虑安全差异。这些假设简化了模型，使问题可解。
steps.hint: 考虑哪些因素可以忽略或标准化，例如天气变化、电池寿命等。

steps.guide_style: 检查符号一致性与单位是否齐全
steps.title: 符号说明与变量定义
steps.prompt: 定义模型中使用的符号和变量，包括：家庭日用电量 $E_{daily}$ (kWh)、电池可用容量 $C_{usable}$ (kWh)、连续功率 $P_{cont}$ (kW)、瞬时功率 $P_{peak}$ (kW)、效率 $\eta$、成本 $Cost$ (USD)。给出每个变量的单位。

criteria.dim: 定义至少5个关键变量，包括单位
符号清晰，与模型一致
变量定义完整，无遗漏

steps.reference_outline: 设 $E_{daily}$ 为家庭日用电量 (kWh)，$C_{usable}$ 为电池可用容量 (kWh)，$P_{cont}$ 为连续功率 (kW)，$P_{peak}$ 为瞬时功率 (kW)，$\eta$ 为充放电效率 (百分比)，$Cost$ 为电池成本 (USD)。另外定义 $N$ 为所需电池数量，$E_{night}$ 为夜间用电量 (kWh)，$E_{cloudy}$ 为阴天额外用电量。
steps.hint: 参考题目表格中的电池规格，如可用容量、连续功率等。

steps.guide_style: 推敲方程是否自洽、可解、贴合题意
steps.title: 模型建立：能量需求模型
steps.prompt: 建立计算家庭日用电量 $E_{daily}$ 的模型。假设家庭有4人，列出典型电器（如冰箱、空调、照明、电视等）的功率和使用时间，计算总用电量。再考虑夜间（12小时）和阴天（连续2天无太阳）的储能需求，得出所需电池总容量 $C_{required}$。

criteria.dim: 列出至少5种电器及其功率和使用时间
正确计算日用电量
正确计算夜间和阴天储能需求
公式推导合理，单位正确

steps.reference_outline: 假设电器：冰箱150W（24h），空调2000W（8h），照明200W（5h），电视100W（4h），其他500W（6h）。日用电量 $E_{daily}=150\times24+2000\times8+200\times5+100\times4+500\times6=3600+16000+1000+400+3000=24000$ Wh = 24 kWh。夜间12小时用电约12 kWh，阴天2天需额外48 kWh，总需求 $C_{required}=12+48=60$ kWh。考虑效率，实际需 $60/\eta$。
steps.hint: 注意单位换算，1 kWh = 1000 Wh。阴天需求可假设为连续2天无太阳。

steps.guide_style: 推敲方程是否自洽、可解、贴合题意
steps.title: 模型建立：电池选型模型
steps.prompt: 基于步骤4的能量需求，建立电池选型模型。考虑成本、容量、功率、效率等因素，定义综合评分函数 $S = w_1 \cdot f_1(Cost) + w_2 \cdot f_2(Capacity) + w_3 \cdot f_3(Power) + w_4 \cdot f_4(\eta)$，其中 $w_i$ 为权重（和为1），$f_i$ 为归一化函数。给出权重建议值。

criteria.dim: 定义综合评分函数，包含至少4个因素
给出归一化方法（如min-max归一化）
给出合理的权重建议并解释
公式完整，逻辑清晰

steps.reference_outline: 综合评分 $S = w_1 \cdot (1 - Cost_{norm}) + w_2 \cdot Capacity_{norm} + w_3 \cdot Power_{norm} + w_4 \cdot \eta_{norm}$，其中 $Cost_{norm} = (Cost - Cost_{min})/(Cost_{max} - Cost_{min})$，其他类似。权重建议：成本0.3，容量0.3，功率0.2，效率0.2。因为成本与容量最重要。
steps.hint: 归一化使不同量纲的指标可比。权重可根据个人偏好调整。

steps.guide_style: 逐步调试，关注可运行性与结果合理
steps.title: 模型求解：计算与选择
steps.prompt: 使用题目表格中的5种电池数据（Deka Solar、Trojan、Discover、Electriq、Tesla），计算每种电池满足能量需求所需的数量 $N = \lceil C_{required} / C_{usable} \rceil$，然后计算总成本 $TotalCost = N \times Cost$，以及综合评分 $S$。选出最优电池。

criteria.dim: 正确计算每种电池所需数量
正确计算总成本和综合评分
选出最优电池并给出理由
计算过程清晰，无错误

steps.reference_outline: 以 $C_{required}=60$ kWh为例：Deka需 $\lceil60/1.18\rceil=51$块，总成本 $51\times368=18768$；Trojan需 $\lceil60/2.5\rceil=24$块，总成本 $24\times492=11808$；Discover需 $\lceil60/7.4\rceil=9$块，总成本 $9\times6478=58302$；Electriq需 $\lceil60/10\rceil=6$块，总成本 $6\times13000=78000$；Tesla需 $\lceil60/13.5\rceil=5$块，总成本 $5\times8500=42500$。综合评分后，Tesla因容量大、效率高、成本适中而最优。
steps.hint: 注意向上取整，因为电池数量必须为整数。

steps.guide_style: 追问结果是否可信、与现实是否吻合
steps.title: 结果分析与检验
steps.prompt: 分析步骤6的结果：检查所选电池是否满足连续功率和瞬时功率需求（假设家庭最大同时功率为10 kW）。若不满足，讨论如何调整（如增加电池数量或选择其他电池）。

criteria.dim: 检查连续功率和瞬时功率是否满足
若满足，说明理由；若不满足，提出调整方案
分析合理，逻辑清晰

steps.reference_outline: Tesla Powerwall+连续功率7 kW，5块总连续功率35 kW，远大于10 kW，满足。瞬时功率10 kW（10秒），5块总瞬时功率50 kW，也满足。因此无需调整。若选Discover，单块连续6.65 kW，9块总59.85 kW，也满足。但总成本更高。
steps.hint: 总功率 = 单块功率 × 电池数量。

steps.guide_style: 引导设计扰动实验并解读
steps.title: 灵敏度分析
steps.prompt: 选择两个关键参数（如日用电量 $E_{daily}$ 和电池效率 $\eta$），分别变化±20%，重新计算最优电池选择，观察结果是否变化。讨论模型的稳定性。

criteria.dim: 选择两个参数进行灵敏度分析
正确计算变化后的结果
讨论模型稳定性，指出敏感参数

steps.reference_outline: 当 $E_{daily}$ 增加20%至28.8 kWh，$C_{required}$ 变为72 kWh，Tesla需6块，总成本51000，仍优于其他。当 $\eta$ 降低20%至0.72，实际需求 $60/0.72=83.3$ kWh，Tesla需7块，总成本59500，此时Discover可能更优（9块58302）。模型对效率较敏感，但对用电量变化较稳定。
steps.hint: 灵敏度分析可以改变参数值，重新计算评分。

steps.guide_style: 强调创新点与清晰表达
steps.title: 模型评价与推广
steps.prompt: 总结模型的优缺点（至少各2点），并讨论如何推广到其他家庭（如不同面积、不同气候）。另外，简要说明如何将水泥电池纳入模型（需哪些额外数据）。

criteria.dim: 列出至少2个优点和2个缺点
讨论模型推广方法
讨论水泥电池的纳入所需数据

steps.reference_outline: 优点：模型简单易用，考虑多个因素；缺点：未考虑电池寿命、维护成本等。推广：可调整用电量公式（如按面积比例）和权重（如寒冷地区更重效率）。水泥电池需数据：容量、功率、效率、成本、寿命、安全性等，才能纳入评分函数。
steps.hint: 水泥电池是新技术，数据可能不完整，需假设或估计。
